sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Increasing fatigue, particularly at the end of the day, and a concern regarding a palpable nodule in his right testicle.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports increasing fatigue, primarily experienced at the end of the day, and attributes it to age and general exhaustion. He continues to ride his bicycle, approximately 20 to 30 miles, though he finds it more challenging than in the past, requiring breaks after longer rides. He notes that his friend, Tyler, is consistently faster than him, which is frustrating. The patient underwent aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair in January 2013. He reports no recent cardiac events or significant changes in his condition. He has been experiencing a palpable nodule in his right testicle for approximately six months, which he brought to our attention. He has not performed routine testicular self-exams but has been prompted to investigate further. He has received both doses of the COVID-19 vaccine. His wife has observed pursed lip breathing during exhalation. He denies any recent trauma or significant changes in his symptoms.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical examination reveals pursed lip breathing during exhalation and a low exhalation phase. The right testicle is palpably enlarged, measuring approximately 2 to 3 millimeters, without signs of hernia. Vital signs are within normal limits. Laboratory results show an elevated alkaline phosphatase level of 156. The patient's complete blood count and thyroid stimulating hormone are within normal limits. Vitamin D levels are at the high end of normal.
Assessment
The patient's primary concerns include increasing fatigue, a palpable testicular nodule, and an elevated alkaline phosphatase level. Clinical evaluation is warranted to determine the etiology of the fatigue and to assess the testicular nodule.
Plan
I am ordering an echocardiogram and a stress test to evaluate cardiac function. I recommend a referral to cardiology with Dr. Vincent Sanchez for further management. A referral to urology has been placed for evaluation of the right testicular nodule. I have ordered pulmonary function testing to evaluate the low exhalation phase observed during the physical exam. I will also order a repeat alkaline phosphatase and complete metabolic panel to investigate the elevated level. Follow-up will be scheduled in approximately one year for a wellness visit, sooner if clinically indicated. The patient should contact the office with any questions or concerns.
